title_en: RuneScape IP Makes the Jump to Console for the First Time in Its 25-Year History With RuneScape: Dragonwilds Later in 2026
summary_en: RuneScape: Dragonwilds launches on PlayStation 5 later in 2026, developer Jagex has announced, marking the first time the RuneScape IP has made the jump from PC to consoles in its 25-year history.
body_en:
RuneScape: Dragonwilds launches on PlayStation 5 later in 2026, developer Jagex has announced, marking the first time the RuneScape IP has made the jump from PC to consoles in its 25-year history.
As confirmed during Sony’s State of Play showcase, RuneScape: Dragonwilds also joins PlayStation Plus Extra and Premium at launch as a day one title.
RuneScape: Dragonwilds is an open-world survival crafting game from Jagex that has already sold 1 million copies on PC via Steam, where it has a ‘very positive’ user review rating. Here’s the official blurb:
The main RuneScape game — the long-running medieval fantasy MMO that launched in 2001 — is available to play on PC and mobile in modern RuneScape and Old School RuneScape form, but neither is on consoles.
Here's everything announced at State of Play June 2026 if you need a catch up on what PlayStation has in store.
Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.
